steps.title: Use the answer-choice structure
steps.content: "140% greater" means the mass is multiplied by $1+1.40=2.40$ each growth period. Since each period is $5$ weeks, the exponent must count 5-week periods: $t/5$.

steps.title: Eliminate by multiplier and exponent
steps.content: The equations with multiplier $1.40$ represent a $40\%$ increase, not a $140\%$ increase. The equation with multiplier $2.40$ and exponent $5/t$ has the right multiplier but decreases as time increases. Only $M=42.75(2.40)^{\frac{t}{5}}$ has both $2.40$ and $t/5$.

steps.title: Quickly verify the starting value
steps.content:
The correct answer says $M=42.75(2.40)^{t/5}$. At $t=15$, this gives:
$$42.75(2.40)^3\approx591.0$$
That matches the given mass.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: $M = 528.30(1.40)^{5/t}$ — Uses multiplier 1.40 (not 2.40) and wrong exponent $\frac{5}{t}$. Eliminated.
  • B: $M = 441.42(2.40)^{5/t}$ — Correct multiplier but wrong exponent direction $\frac{5}{t}$; also wrong initial mass. Eliminated.
  • C: $M = 215.38(1.40)^{t/5}$ — Uses multiplier 1.40 instead of 2.40. Eliminated.
  • D: $M = 42.75(2.40)^{t/5}$ — Multiplier 2.40 and exponent $\frac{t}{5}$ are both correct, and $M_0 \approx 42.75$ matches our calculation. That confirms D is correct.
